title: Metastelma schlechteri
intro: Metastelma schlechteri is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family (Apocynaceae), commonly known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Metastelma schlechteri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Metastelma schlechteri is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Metastelma schlechteri is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Madagascar. This plant is classified in the genus Metastelma, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as 'dogbanes' or 'climbing dogbanes'. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes oleander, periwinkle, and mandrake. Metastelma schlechteri is often found growing in woodland and scrubland habitats.
